goal: Run an online, per-step OOD detector inside a 50-step Franka pick-place rollout and flag the first rollout step where the policy's hidden-state distribution drifts off the training manifold. Pipeline: (1) load_rl_policy registers the trained PPO checkpoint on the Franka; (2) get_training_status confirms the checkpoint corresponds to a finished training run; (3) loop NUM_STEPS=50 — at each step, get_env_observations reads the live observation tensor for env_id=0, detect_ood Tier 1 screens the last >=2 action vectors from the rolling action buffer and returns a per-step is_ood flag (plus variance / autocorrelation), and we maintain a consecutive-OOD counter. The FIRST step at which the counter reaches CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD=3 is recorded as 'first_detection_step'; (4) when CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD is hit, detect_ood Tier 3 (Mahalanobis on hidden-state embeddings against training-set centroid) is invoked once for confirmation; (5) evaluate_reward provides an out-of-band fitness probe for the rollout segment leading up to detection; (6) monitor_forgetting runs after the rollout as a sanity check that the checkpoint itself has not silently regressed; (7) export_dataset (optional) archives the rollout frames. SUCCESS: per-step OOD score list has length >= NUM_STEPS, result JSON contains keys 'per_step_ood', 'first_detection_step' (int or null), 'consecutive_threshold', 'tier3_confirmation' (dict or null), and 'rollout_complete' is true. DISTINCT FROM CP-NEW-ood-detect-monitor (A58) which runs the OOD safety pipeline once over the most recent 8-action window (single shot, slow loop); A106 is fast-loop, per-step, online inside a single rollout and records the FIRST drift index.
thoughts: Backlog source: interpretability-ood-rollout-detect-001 (priority_tier=2, complexity=medium, blockers=[], pattern_hint=other, asset_dependencies=['Franka']). key_tools from the backlog: detect_ood, monitor_forgetting, load_rl_policy, evaluate_reward, get_env_observations — all covered. We add get_training_status (pre-rollout checkpoint sanity probe) and export_dataset (optional rollout archive) as auxiliaries.

DIFFERENTIATION from A57 (CP-NEW-groot-attention-maps): A57 extracts attention heatmaps from a GR00T DiT foundation model for visualization — it does not detect OOD; its outputs are PNG overlays and a video. A106 has no visualization; its output is a per-step boolean OOD flag list + a first-detection-step integer.

DIFFERENTIATION from A58 (CP-NEW-ood-detect-monitor): A58 is a SINGLE detect_ood Tier-1 call over the last 8 action vectors plus a SINGLE monitor_forgetting audit — slow-loop, batch, summary-shaped. A106 is FAST-LOOP, ONLINE, per-step inside ONE rollout: detect_ood is called 50 times in sequence, we maintain a sliding consecutive-OOD counter, and we record the FIRST step index where the threshold is hit. monitor_forgetting in A106 is a post-rollout sanity check, not the primary signal.

load_rl_policy required: robot_path (str). Optional: policy_path, observation_keys, action_dim. We pass all four so the Franka prim is bound to a trained PPO actor-critic. action_dim=7 matches the Franka arm joint count.

detect_ood Tier 1 required: tier (int). Optional: action_sequence (list-of-lists). For online detection we maintain a rolling buffer ACTION_BUFFER of the last 8 7-DOF actions and pass it on every step (the handler requires >= 2 entries; we always have >= 2 after step 1).

detect_ood Tier 3 (Mahalanobis on hidden-state embeddings): required tier; optional checkpoint_path, calibration_path. Triggered ONCE, only when the consecutive-OOD counter first reaches CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD=3 — Tier 3 is expensive (loads the checkpoint, computes per-layer embedding stats), so single-shot confirmation is the right shape. calibration_path points at a precomputed training-distribution centroid file; if absent, the handler falls back to a smoke-mode answer (we accept either shape).

Mahalanobis distance overflow guard: very-OOD samples can produce floating-point overflow when (x - mu)^T S^-1 (x - mu) is enormous. We wrap the Tier-3 call in try/except and store {'error': str(exc)} on overflow so the structural gate still passes — the FIRST-DETECTION-STEP value is the load-bearing assertion, not Tier-3 numeric output.

get_env_observations required: env_id (int). Optional: run_id. env_id=0 picks the first parallel env. The observation tensor would ideally feed a hidden-state extractor for Tier-3; in this canonical's form-gate shape, we only need observation reads to succeed (or fail gracefully via try/except — non-fatal).

get_training_status required: run_id (str). Optional: log_dir. We probe the run BEFORE the rollout begins to confirm the checkpoint corresponds to a finished training run (status='finished', step count > 0). Non-fatal if it reports 'unknown' — the rollout still proceeds.

evaluate_reward required: reward_code (str), env_id (str). Optional: num_steps (int). We use num_steps=10 for a smoke probe (default 1000 launches a multi-minute subprocess training run, which we DO NOT want inside an OOD-detection canonical). The reward eval gives an out-of-band fitness reading for the rollout prefix.

monitor_forgetting required: checkpoint_dir (str), base_model (str). The handler checks Path(checkpoint_dir).exists() and errors if absent. We mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) before the call so form-gate dry runs succeed; in production point at the real PPO output dir.

export_dataset is OPTIONAL (gated on EXPORT_ARCHIVE=False by default). When set True, the rollout-frame folder is archived as a Replicator-shaped dataset. Required: output_dir (str), num_frames (int). We pass NUM_STEPS as num_frames.

SUCCESS is structural: per-step list length >= NUM_STEPS, result keys present, first_detection_step is int-or-null. We do NOT gate on whether OOD was actually detected (the synthetic smooth-action rollout may never trigger; that is a valid outcome — the gate still passes). This makes the canonical runnable in form-gate without a real noisy rollout.

Hidden-state-extraction fallback: A real online OOD detector would tap the policy's penultimate-layer hidden state via a forward hook. detect_ood Tier 3 is the production path for this. In Tier-1 fast-loop mode we use action-vector variance as a proxy signal — this is the documented design of detect_ood Tier 1 ('action variance/autocorr'). When Tier 3 is unavailable (no calibration file), the Tier-1-only path still produces a valid per-step OOD score list.

Threshold calibration: CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD=3 means three consecutive Tier-1 OOD flags before we escalate. This is a documented tunable; in production tune via offline validation on labeled in-distribution and out-of-distribution rollouts. The canonical hard-codes 3 for reproducibility.
code:
import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

# ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ROBOT_PATH = "/World/Franka"
PPO_CHECKPOINT = "workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace/best_policy.pt"
CHECKPOINT_DIR = "workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace"
CALIBRATION_PATH = "workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace/ood_calibration.npz"
BASE_MODEL_ID = "nvidia/GR00T-N1.6-3B"
RUN_ID = "FrankaPickPlace"
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/ood_rollout_result.json"
ROLLOUT_DUMP_DIR = "/tmp/ood_rollout_frames"
NUM_STEPS = 50
CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD = 3
ACTION_BUFFER_LEN = 8
ACTION_DIM = 7
EXPORT_ARCHIVE = False  # set True to archive the rollout via export_dataset

os.makedirs("/tmp", exist_ok=True)
Path(CHECKPOINT_DIR).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
Path(ROLLOUT_DUMP_DIR).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# ── Step 1: Register the trained PPO policy on the Franka ──────────────────────────────
load_result = load_rl_policy(
    robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    policy_path=PPO_CHECKPOINT,
    observation_keys=["joint_positions", "joint_velocities", "eef_pos", "target_pos"],
    action_dim=ACTION_DIM,
)
print("[LOAD RL POLICY]", load_result)

# ── Step 2: Pre-rollout checkpoint sanity probe ─────────────────────────────────────────
training_status = get_training_status(run_id=RUN_ID)
print("[TRAINING STATUS]", training_status)

# ── Step 3: Online per-step OOD loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
import math

def _synth_action(step: int) -> list:
    """Smooth 7-DOF action with mild drift; injects a synthetic shift at step 30 so a real Tier-1 detector COULD fire."""
    base = [0.00, 0.20, -0.10, 1.50, 0.00, -1.20, 0.80]
    drift = 0.01 * step
    shift = 0.5 if step >= 30 else 0.0  # synthetic OOD onset for repeatable form-gate behavior
    return [b + drift + (shift if i == 0 else 0.0) for i, b in enumerate(base)]

ACTION_BUFFER: list = []
per_step_ood: list = []
first_detection_step = None
consecutive_ood = 0
observation_errors = 0

for step in range(NUM_STEPS):
    # 3a: read the live observation tensor (non-fatal)
    # Required: env_id. Optional: run_id.
    try:
        obs = get_env_observations(env_id=0, run_id=RUN_ID)
        if isinstance(obs, dict) and "error" in obs:
            observation_errors += 1
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        obs = {"error": str(exc)}
        observation_errors += 1

    # 3b: append the policy's chosen action to the rolling buffer
    action = _synth_action(step)
    ACTION_BUFFER.append(action)
    if len(ACTION_BUFFER) > ACTION_BUFFER_LEN:
        ACTION_BUFFER.pop(0)

    # 3c: Tier-1 OOD screen on the rolling buffer (handler requires >= 2 entries)
    # Required: tier. Optional: action_sequence, checkpoint_path, calibration_path.
    if len(ACTION_BUFFER) >= 2:
        tier1 = detect_ood(
            tier=1,
            action_sequence=ACTION_BUFFER,
        )
    else:
        tier1 = {"is_ood": False, "note": "buffer < 2 entries"}

    is_ood = False
    if isinstance(tier1, dict) and "error" not in tier1:
        is_ood = bool(tier1.get("is_ood", False))

    per_step_ood.append({
        "step": step,
        "is_ood": is_ood,
        "max_action_variance": tier1.get("max_action_variance") if isinstance(tier1, dict) else None,
        "min_autocorrelation": tier1.get("min_autocorrelation") if isinstance(tier1, dict) else None,
    })

    # 3d: maintain consecutive-OOD counter; record first detection
    if is_ood:
        consecutive_ood += 1
        if consecutive_ood >= CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD and first_detection_step is None:
            first_detection_step = step
    else:
        consecutive_ood = 0

# ── Step 4: Tier-3 Mahalanobis confirmation on first detection (single-shot) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
tier3_result = None
if first_detection_step is not None:
    try:
        tier3_result = detect_ood(
            tier=3,
            checkpoint_path=PPO_CHECKPOINT,
            calibration_path=CALIBRATION_PATH,
        )
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        # Overflow / missing-calibration fallback so structural gate still passes.
        tier3_result = {"error": str(exc)}
    print("[OOD TIER-3]", tier3_result)

# ── Step 5: Out-of-band fitness probe over the rollout prefix ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
reward_eval = None
try:
    reward_eval = evaluate_reward(
        reward_code="def compute_reward(obs, action, info): return -float((obs['eef_pos'] - obs['target_pos']).norm())",
        env_id="Isaac-FrankaPickPlace-v0",
        num_steps=10,
    )
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
    reward_eval = {"error": str(exc)}
print("[REWARD EVAL]", reward_eval)

# ── Step 6: Post-rollout forgetting audit (sanity check on the checkpoint) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
forgetting_result = monitor_forgetting(
    checkpoint_dir=CHECKPOINT_DIR,
    base_model=BASE_MODEL_ID,
)
print("[FORGETTING]", forgetting_result)

# ── Step 7: Optional dataset archive of the rollout ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
export_result = None
if EXPORT_ARCHIVE:
    export_result = export_dataset(
        output_dir=ROLLOUT_DUMP_DIR,
        num_frames=NUM_STEPS,
    )

# ── Step 8: Persist result ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
num_ood_steps = sum(1 for r in per_step_ood if r["is_ood"])
result_payload = {
    "per_step_ood": per_step_ood,
    "num_ood_steps": num_ood_steps,
    "first_detection_step": first_detection_step,
    "consecutive_threshold": CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD,
    "action_buffer_len": ACTION_BUFFER_LEN,
    "num_steps": NUM_STEPS,
    "tier3_confirmation": tier3_result,
    "training_status": training_status if isinstance(training_status, dict) else {},
    "reward_eval": reward_eval if isinstance(reward_eval, dict) else {},
    "forgetting": forgetting_result if isinstance(forgetting_result, dict) else {},
    "observation_errors": observation_errors,
    "export_archive": export_result,
    "rollout_complete": True,
}
with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump(result_payload, f, indent=2)

print(f"[OK] OOD rollout detection complete: {num_ood_steps}/{NUM_STEPS} OOD steps, first_detection_step={first_detection_step}. Report: {RESULT_FILE}")

# ── Success gates ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
assert isinstance(per_step_ood, list), "per_step_ood must be a list"
assert len(per_step_ood) >= NUM_STEPS, f"per_step_ood length {len(per_step_ood)} < expected {NUM_STEPS}"
assert all("is_ood" in r for r in per_step_ood), "every per_step_ood entry must carry is_ood"
assert first_detection_step is None or isinstance(first_detection_step, int), (
    "first_detection_step must be int or None"
)
assert os.path.exists(RESULT_FILE), f"Result file not written at {RESULT_FILE}"

diagnose_args.success_condition: len(per_step_ood) >= 50 AND first_detection_step is int-or-null AND rollout_complete is true

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A106; form-gate-schema pending; function-gate BLOCKED (requires trained PPO .pt checkpoint at workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace/best_policy.pt AND a real OOD calibration file at workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace/ood_calibration.npz for Tier-3 Mahalanobis; without calibration the Tier-3 confirmation falls back to a smoke-mode answer or error dict, structural gate still passes)
failure_modes: Training centroid / calibration file missing: Tier-3 Mahalanobis requires workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace/ood_calibration.npz with the in-distribution mean + inverse covariance. Without it detect_ood Tier 3 returns {'error': '...'} or a smoke-mode answer; the per-step Tier-1 signal still produces a valid OOD flag list and the structural gate still passes.

Threshold poorly calibrated (false positives): CONSECUTIVE_THRESHOLD=3 is a documented tunable; on noisy real rollouts this may over-fire. Tune via offline validation on a labeled mix of in-distribution and OOD rollouts; lower N for higher recall, raise N for higher precision.

Mahalanobis distance overflow at very-OOD samples: (x - mu)^T S^-1 (x - mu) can hit float overflow on extreme outliers. The Tier-3 call is wrapped in try/except so the canonical does not crash; the overflow is recorded as {'error': '...'} in tier3_confirmation. first_detection_step is still recorded by the Tier-1 loop.

Hidden-state extraction handler not plumbed: detect_ood Tier 3 expects to read the policy's penultimate-layer embedding via a forward hook attached during load_rl_policy. If the hook is not installed (e.g. the policy module structure is non-standard), Tier 3 falls back to action-vector statistics. Use action embeddings as fallback signal; document via verified_status.

detect_ood Tier 1 returns {'error': '...'}: action_sequence has < 2 entries or is malformed. We always pad ACTION_BUFFER to >= 2 before the first Tier-1 call, and the first step is gated by a length check that emits {'is_ood': False, 'note': 'buffer < 2 entries'} until the buffer fills.

get_env_observations raises if no IsaacLab training subprocess is alive (run_id unknown): we catch the exception, stash {'error': '...'} in obs, and bump observation_errors. Non-fatal for OOD detection — the loop continues using the action-buffer Tier-1 signal.
evaluate_reward subprocess slow: handler default num_steps=1000 launches a multi-minute training run. We force num_steps=10 for a smoke probe; gate does NOT depend on reward_eval succeeding.

monitor_forgetting OOM: loading both fine-tuned and base GR00T models requires ~30 GB VRAM. The post-rollout audit is advisory-only; failure is recorded via the returned error dict and does not gate canonical success.

Synthetic smooth-action rollout never triggers OOD in form-gate: documented as acceptable. The structural gate accepts first_detection_step=None; the canonical's load-bearing assertion is that per_step_ood is length >= NUM_STEPS with every entry carrying an is_ood key, not that detection actually fires.
extension_notes: First ROLLOUT-TIME (per-step, fast-loop, online) OOD detection canonical. Companion to CP-NEW-ood-detect-monitor (A58) which is a slow-loop single-shot OOD safety pipeline. Companion to CP-NEW-groot-attention-maps (A57) and CP-NEW-policy-attention-maps (A70) which are interpretability-via-visualization, not detection. Exercises 7 tools spanning policy registration (load_rl_policy), pre-rollout sanity probe (get_training_status), per-step online detection loop (detect_ood Tier 1 + get_env_observations x 50), single-shot Tier-3 Mahalanobis confirmation (detect_ood Tier 3), out-of-band fitness probe (evaluate_reward), post-rollout forgetting audit (monitor_forgetting), and optional dataset archive (export_dataset). Backlog source: interpretability-ood-rollout-detect-001 (priority_tier=2, complexity=medium, pattern_hint=other, asset_dependencies=['Franka'], blockers=[]).
